brief_justification: Arrives at exact correct answer (No) with valid multi-layered reasoning separating selection mechanism from causal null claim; traces second/third-order effects including the distinction between aversiveness predicting dropout vs. efficacy among completers; shows some structural awareness in the table format but lacks explicit named bias-checking or mid-analysis course correction; provides the cortisol-in-completers test as a de facto verification path but stops short of explicit counterfactual or boundary condition testing; cleanly distinguishes what data supports vs. what it leaves untested using 'necessary but not sufficient' framing; presents a table of three explicitly named competing causal structures and explains why all predict the same dropout pattern; every conclusion traces to a derivation with the three-model table and logical gap section enabling full third-party reproduction; explicitly avoids treating dropout confirmation as proof of causation and articulates why that inference fails; zero evidence of correlation-to-causation leap, single-hypothesis commitment, or linear commitment — all key suppressed failure modes avoided; follows structured reasoning with implicit branches (three causal models) and gates (what would vs. wouldn't test the claim), but does not explicitly map to the S1→G1→S3→S4→S5→G2 topology steps.
